title: Verification Gates — US
description: 20 verification gates total as of v0.3.0: G1-G10 inherited from china-equity-ic-rigor, G11-G14 US-specific from delta matrix Layer 10, G15-G17 added v0.2.0 (Sprint 1: consensus variance, bank discipline, revision velocity), G18-G20 added v0.3.0 (Sprint 2 rigor hardening: cross-doc Barra consistency, Plugin-1-to-Plugin-2 provenance, view defensibility). A memo cannot claim score >8.0 with any non-n_a gate failing. Run via Phase C scripts; results captured here for human-readable audit trail. Schema accepts 0.1.0 (14-gate), 0.2.0 (17-gate), and 0.3.0 (20-gate) for grandfathering.

gate_status.description: pass = gate verified; fail = blocks scoring above 8.0; n_a = gate not applicable (e.g. single-segment company → no segment GM gate); skipped = explicitly skipped by user with documented reason.

verification_script.description: Path to script that produced this verification, e.g. 'scripts/verify_eps_pe.py'

manual_check_note.description: If gate verified manually (not via script), how it was checked

description.description: What this gate checks and why it matters

remediation_required.description: If status=fail, what to fix and where (file:section)

category.description: Bug category. math=arithmetic; source=citation/anchor; consistency=cross-section; structure=missing required component; quant_overlay=B13-B14 US-specific

blocks_score_above.description: Score cap if this gate fails. Default 8.0 for most gates; 7.0 for critical math gates G1/G3.

run_id.description: Unique ID for this verification run, e.g. 'NVDA_2026-05-15_run3'

memo_path.description: Path to the IC memo this verification applies to

memo_structured_path.description: Path to the structured JSON conforming to schemas/memo.json

gates.description: Gate count depends on schema_version: v0.1.0 = exactly 14 (G1-G14); v0.2.0 = exactly 17 (G1-G17 with G15-G17 = consensus_variance, bank_metrics, revision_velocity); v0.3.0 = exactly 20 (G1-G20 with G18 = quant cross-doc consistency, G19 = Plugin-1-to-Plugin-2 provenance, G20 = view defensibility). All declared gates must appear in the array (even if status=n_a). Verifier scripts enforce the exact-count constraint per schema_version.

overall_pass.description: True iff all gates are pass or n_a. Any 'fail' status sets overall_pass=false.

blocks_score_above.description: Min of gate-level blocks_score_above for any failing gate. If overall_pass=true, this is 10.0 (no cap).

gate_definitions.description: Reference catalog. Listed here for completeness — the actual gates are in the 'gates' array.

G1.const: EPS × Multiple multiplicativity — for every scenario row in every valuation table, verify EPS × multiple = target_price within rounding (±0.5%). Verified by scripts/verify_eps_pe.py. Most-failed gate.

G2.const: Segment GM reconciliation — Σ(segment_revenue × segment_GM) / Σ(segment_revenue) within ±50bp of consolidated GM as reported. Verified by scripts/verify_segment_gm.py. n_a for single-segment companies.

G3.const: SOTP monotonicity — for every segment in the SOTP table, NI ≤ OP ≤ GP ≤ Revenue. Any inversion fails. Verified by scripts/verify_sotp_monotonicity.py.

G4.const: Scenario probabilities sum to 1.00 ±0.01. Verified by scripts/verify_scenario_weights.py.

G5.const: Bear/bull EPS bridge reconciles to base — sum of named adjustments must equal (base_eps - scenario_eps) within rounding. Verified by scripts/verify_bear_bridge.py.

G6.const: Source tag declared at first use — every specific number (revenue, GM, segment share, customer concentration, capacity, ADV, beta) tagged S1-S5 or Pending at first appearance in memo Markdown. Verified by scripts/verify_source_tags.py via regex scan.

G7.const: Headline conditionality matches anchor strength — if any top-3 anchor is S3 or weaker, headline contains conditional language ('source-conditional', 'if [trigger]', 'subject to'). If all top-3 are S1-S2, headline can be unconditional. Verified by scripts/verify_headline_conditionality.py.

G8.const: GM taxonomy box exists — somewhere in memo, the 5 GM types (T1 consolidated / T2 segment / T3 sub-segment / T4 analyst-modeled / T5 marginal) are explicitly defined and each GM mention in the memo is implicitly tagged to one type. Verified by scripts/verify_gm_taxonomy.py.

G9.const: What-would-reverse has numerical denominators — every directional trigger has a numerical threshold, not a handwave. E.g. 'if DC revenue Q4 < $X B' not 'if DC weakens'. Verified by scripts/verify_what_would_reverse.py.

G10.const: Anchor weighting impact table exists — headline impact of ±10pp probability shift on the strongest scenario is shown. Verified by scripts/verify_weighting_sensitivity.py via JSON presence check.

G11.const: [US-specific, B11] Non-GAAP to GAAP reconciliation present — every non-GAAP number cited in memo has its GAAP counterpart shown, with the bridge (SBC, restructuring, amortization of acquired intangibles, etc.) listed. Verified by scripts/verify_non_gaap.py. n_a if no non-GAAP cited.

G12.const: [US-specific, B12] SBC treatment in FCF disclosed — memo states whether SBC is deducted from FCF; if not deducted, dilution effect from RSU/option vest is shown separately. Common bug: 'FCF' cited as bull case without flagging SBC as a real cost. Verified by scripts/verify_fcf_definition.py. n_a if no FCF cited.

G13.const: [US-specific, B13] Factor exposure stated — Barra-style factor tags (Value / Quality / Momentum / Growth / Size / Low-Vol / Liquidity) appear in §11 quant overlay. Required in every institutional IC memo per D13. Verified by scripts/verify_quant_overlay.py.

G14.const: [US-specific, B14] Capacity / ADV / days-to-exit stated — for the recommended position size, days-to-exit at 10%/20%/30% participation against name's 30-day ADV is shown. Required per D13. Verified by scripts/verify_quant_overlay.py.

G15.const: [v0.2.0] Consensus variance — for any non-Hold rating (Strong Buy / Buy / Sell / Strong Sell), memo must declare ≥1 load-bearing entry in source_tags consensus_variance block: type ∈ {revenue, margin, multiple, scenario_weight, timing}, ≥1 evidence_ref at S1-S3 (not all-S4 or all-S5), sizing_impact_pp ≥ 2.0. Hold ratings AND self-labeled 'consensus-anchored' memos in headline = n_a. Names with n_analysts<5 = n_a. Verified by scripts/verify_consensus_variance.py. blocks_score_above = 7.0 on fail. Full discipline in references/consensus-variance-us.md.

G16.const: [v0.2.0] Bank discipline — for sector ∈ {Financials/Banks, Diversified Banks, Regional Banks, Investment Banking & Brokerage, Insurance, BDC}, memo must contain all four required disclosures: (a) AOCI bridge (AFS book vs fair value + AOCI mark + HTM unrealized loss + MTM TBVPS), (b) CET1 walk (period-over-period bridge), (c) NIM trajectory (5y + deposit beta), (d) Stress capital context (SCB + capital return capacity + CCAR/DFAST trough). n_a for non-bank sectors. Verified by scripts/verify_bank_metrics.py. blocks_score_above = 7.0 on fail. Full discipline in phase-1-deep-dive-us.md §FS-Banks Augmentation.

G17.const: [v0.2.0] Revision velocity — memo must declare in source_tags revision_velocity block: 3-month FY1 EPS revision % + direction + breadth ((up-down)/N). Optional but recommended: 1m and 6m windows, FY2 revision, PT revision, pre-print drift, peer median comparison. n_a if n_analysts < 5 (revision velocity not load-bearing for thin coverage). Verified by scripts/verify_revision_velocity.py. blocks_score_above = 7.5 on fail. Discipline in phase-2-continuation-us.md §A6 Earnings Revision Velocity subsection.

G18.const: [v0.3.0] Quant overlay cross-document consistency — within a single memo, the structured quant_overlay.factor_tags block in memo.json must match any Markdown narrative reference to the same Barra factor within ±0.2 tolerance. Closes the audit finding that NVDA's Momentum z-score appeared as +1.8 in quant-overlay-us.md but +2.3 in position-sizing-us.md (pre-v0.3.0 example files). Catches authors who quote z-scores in prose that diverge from structured block — common LLM failure mode where the prose is regenerated without re-reading structured data. n_a if no Markdown factor references found. Verified by scripts/verify_quant_cross_doc_consistency.py. blocks_score_above = 7.5 on fail. Discipline in us-equity-ic-rigor/references/quant-overlay-us.md §'Honest framing' (v0.3.0 disclaimer).

G19.const: [v0.3.0] Plugin 1 to Plugin 2 provenance — memo_metadata must reference an outputs/<ticker>_manifest.json file produced by Plugin 1's orchestrator at end of Phase 3, containing run_id (UUID), agent_provenance (hash per specialist output), web_search_log (list of url+timestamp+response_hash), phase_timing. Plugin 2 gate runner verifies manifest exists, contains all required fields, and that verification_calls_count >= 12 per D9. Memos without manifest accepted via explicit --hand-authored flag with WARNING printed in score block and blocks_score_above capped at 7.5. Closes audit finding that Plugin 1 to Plugin 2 handoff is pure filename convention with no provenance enforcement. Verified by scripts/verify_provenance_manifest.py. blocks_score_above = 7.5 on fail (or absent manifest without hand-authored flag).

G20.const: [v0.3.0] View defensibility — for any rubric score above 8.5, three conjunctive conditions must hold: (a) headline expected_return differs from S4 consensus PT-implied return by >= 8 percentage points absolute; (b) at least one declared consensus_variance entry has >=1 evidence_ref at S1 or S2 (not S3, not S4); (c) the memo's adjudication_trail contains at least one entry where R-v2 attempted a structured attack against the declared variance per the 5-point attack checklist in pm-synthesis-adjudication-us.md and the attack was rebutted with documented reasoning. Pushes the rubric from grading structural completeness to grading view survival. Caps score at 8.5 on fail (not 7.0 — G20 is rubric-discriminating not memo-killing). n_a for memos with rating=Hold or with headline self-labeled 'consensus-anchored'. Verified by scripts/verify_view_defensibility.py.
